chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 488 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Application in disposed of case For orders on MA No.4938/2025 (R/Surety) 10.10.2025 Mr. Muhammad Raza Katohar, Deputy Prosecutor General..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- This application has been filed by the applicant seeking return of surety document. As per contents of the application, supported by report of Accountant of this Court, it appears that accused Ghulam Asghar (referred to as Ali Asghar in the FIR) was granted interim pre-arrest bail by this Court vide order dated 05.06.2025, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/-, for which the applicant stood surety by depositing an original defence savings certificate of such amount. Thereafter, the bail application of accused was dismissed by this Court vide order dated 28.08.2025. On the last date of hearing, learned Counsel for the applicant required time to submit document as to appearance of the accused before the learned trial Court, who said to have been appeared and surrendered thereafter for taking into custody. Though no one is present for the applicant today, but the Court’s file shows that a statement dated 29.09.2025 has been filed by the learned Counsel for the applicant, whereby certified copy of case diary dated 30.08.2025 has been furnished, wherein it has been mentioned that the accused had filed an application for surrendering himself before the learned trial Court and he was remanded to jail custody accordingly. Learned DPG, in view of the above position, extends his no objection for granting this application.

Though no one is present for the applicant today, but the Court’s file shows that a statement dated 29.09.2025 has been filed by the learned Counsel for the applicant, whereby certified copy of case diary dated 30.08.2025 has been furnished, wherein it has been mentioned that the accused had filed an application for surrendering himself before the learned trial Court and he was remanded to jail custody accordingly. Learned DPG, in view of the above position, extends his no objection for granting this application. Accordingly, the application is allowed. Office is directed to return the surety document to the applicant after proper identification and verification, as per rules. J U D G E Abdul Basit
